methods.description: RT-PCR analysis was performed to specifically amplify AtkdsA1 and AtkdsA2 cDNAs, with Actin2 gene as a control, to ensure the reaction was in the linear range of PCR amplification and to check for DNA contamination.

steps.description: Specific amplification for AtkdsA1 and AtkdsA2 cDNAs was obtained using specific primers.
steps.searchable_summary: use specific primers to amplify AtkdsA1 and AtkdsA2 cDNAs

steps.description: As a control of RT-PCR expression, a 304 bp cDNA fragment for the Actin2 gene (AtACTIN2) was amplified using a specific set of primers.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify Actin2 gene cDNA fragment as RT-PCR control

steps.description: After an initial denaturation step of 5 min at 95°C, the reaction programme was as follows: 30 s at 95°C, 30 s at 50°C, and 30 s at 72°C for 33 cycles for AtkdsA1 and AtkdsA2 or 25 cycles for AtACTIN2, and a final step of 5 min at 72°C.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR with specified temperature cycles

steps.description: The amount of first-strand cDNA and the number of cycles allowed the reaction to be in the linear range of PCR amplification.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure PCR reaction is in linear amplification range

steps.description: As a control for DNA contamination, a PCR reaction was performed using the AtACTIN2-specific primers in the absence of any added DNA.
steps.searchable_summary: perform control PCR to check for DNA contamination
